text.title: Technical SEO Foundation
text.summary: robots.txt configuration, XML sitemap structure, HTTPS setup, and redirect rules — the crawlability prerequisites.

section.title: robots.txt Rules

rule.statement: Block admin paths, session ID parameters, and internal APIs; explicitly allow public API paths; add Sitemap directives; never block CSS or JS files.
rule.rationale: Blocking CSS/JS prevents Google from rendering pages correctly, causing ranking loss. Unblocked session URLs create infinite crawl loops that waste crawl budget on non-indexable content.

rule.statement: Configure AI crawlers (GPTBot, Claude-Web, PerplexityBot) separately from search crawlers — allow public content, disallow premium/paywalled content.
rule.rationale: AI crawlers serve a different purpose than search crawlers: they build training data and citation sources, not indexes. Blocking them entirely sacrifices AI search visibility; blocking only premium content is the balanced approach.

section.reference: robots.txt template with AI crawler rules and sitemap directive

section.title: XML Sitemap Rules

rule.statement: Include only indexable pages in the sitemap; update lastmod only when content actually changes; use sitemap index for sites with more than 50,000 URLs.
rule.rationale: Sitemaps with stale lastmod dates or non-indexable URLs train Google's crawler to distrust the sitemap, reducing crawl frequency for the whole site.

rule.statement: Include hreflang alternate links inside sitemap entries for multilingual sites.
rule.rationale: Sitemap hreflang is a fallback for pages where HTML head injection is complex (SPAs, CDN-level rendering). Redundancy in hreflang signals is safe and preferred.

section.reference: XML sitemap template with hreflang alternates

rule.statement: Redirect HTTP to HTTPS and non-www to www (or vice versa) with 301 permanent redirects; avoid redirect chains longer than one hop.
rule.rationale: Each redirect hop loses approximately 10-15% of PageRank. Chains of three or more hops can be abandoned by crawlers entirely, causing indexing failures for the destination page.

list.item: SSL certificate installed and covering all subdomains (wildcard preferred)
No mixed content: all resources (images, scripts, fonts) load over HTTPS
Single canonical domain enforced via server-level redirect
